frameworks.name: information-flow analysis of cortical areas
frameworks.one_line: characterize PF subareas by their unique input-output transformations

frameworks.name: psychological-labelling of cortical function
frameworks.one_line: assigning psychological terms to areas instead of describing operations

frameworks.name: abstract transformation / generalization
frameworks.one_line: mid-dorsal and ventral PF encode transformations abstracted from specific actions or objects

core_question: How does the prefrontal cortex operate, described in terms of input-output transformations rather than psychological labels?
key_assumptions: function follows from anatomical input-output patterns
each PF subarea has a unique pattern of connections
abstract transformations support generalization to novel problems
manipulations: comparison across three PF subregions: caudal, mid-dorsal, ventral
key_findings: caudal PF transforms attended or short-term-memory input into eye-movement targets
mid-dorsal PF transforms object/action order into eye- and hand-movement sequences
ventral PF transforms object or sound input into prospective associated representations
mid-dorsal and ventral PF encode abstract transformations independent of specific actions or objects
interpretations: PF function is best described by area-specific input-output transformations
abstract encoding enables generalization to novel problems sharing underlying logic
